evals.name: scaffold-orders-module
evals.prompt: Adding a new feature module `feature/orders` to our KMP/Compose Multiplatform project. Need to list user's orders, view single order details, see order status. Following our compose-arch convention. Walk through the scaffolding step by step — module structure, build.gradle.kts dependencies, layered files (Screen/View/Component/UseCase/Repository), typed error sealed class, and what to verify at the end (build, tests). Don't do the full implementation — show the scaffolding workflow and what each file looks like at minimum.
evals.expected_output: Sequential workflow: create module dir, build.gradle.kts with KMP targets and required deps, layered files with stubs, sealed OrderError, build verification step explicitly mentioned.
assertions: Output is procedural: numbered or sequential steps mirroring skill workflow
Lists exact module directory tree (e.g. feature/orders/src/commonMain/kotlin/...)
Provides build.gradle.kts with KMP targets and required deps
All compose-arch layers stubbed (Screen, View, Component, UseCase, Repository)
Defines a sealed OrderError (typed errors) class
Final step explicitly mentions build verification (./gradlew build or :feature:orders:build)
Mentions tests or test stubs (commonTest source set)

evals.name: add-settings-module
evals.prompt: Spinning up a Settings feature for our KMP app — toggle dark mode, change language, log out. Use our standard feature slice scaffolding workflow. Show the procedural steps a junior could follow without thinking about it. Include where errors get typed (logout can fail), how the Component wires to UseCases, and the build verification step.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step scaffolding: module dir, build script, all layers with stubs, typed errors for fallible ops (logout), explicit build/verify step.
assertions: Procedural sequenced steps (not a freeform essay)
Logout (fallible op) has typed error sealed class
Component wires to UseCases (constructor injection visible)
Build verification step explicitly called out
